title: Electron Energy Loss Spectroscopy (EELS)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical electron-microscopy literature for this principle.

E.description: Electron Energy Loss Spectroscopy (EELS): inelastic electron spectrum produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.transmit_sample -> L.energy_dispersion -> D.eels_spectrum -> int.spectral, with spectral-channel integration and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D eels hyperspectral. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~14; calibration-level mismatch (zero_loss_drift, plural_scattering, edge_overlap) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: S(E, r) = integral [PSF conv density_of_states(E, r)] + plural_scat + zero_loss + n
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter (density / orientation / spectrum / complex field depending on principle)
E.observation_y: Electron detector output at declared geometry + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D eels hyperspectral is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 14); zero_loss_drift dominates the stability cliff; plural_scattering and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Power-Law-BG, MLLS] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [EELS-UNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |zero_loss_drift| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * N_spectral_bins * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
